faqs.question: How much does lawn mowing cost in Seattle, WA?
faqs.answer: Lawn mowing in Seattle costs $75 per visit on average, with most homeowners paying $65–$90 for a standard residential lawn. Monthly plans typically run $260–$320. Seattle's mild climate extends the growing season. Moss, rain garden management, and drought-tolerant tree-service are common requests. Pricing varies by lot size — small lots (under 5,000 sq ft) run lower; large properties (10,000+ sq ft) run higher.

faqs.question: How much does tree-service cost in Seattle?
faqs.answer: Tree Service projects in Seattle vary widely by scope. Basic cleanup and mulch runs $580–$1160. A mid-size front yard redesign costs $3,000–$9,500. Full backyard transformation with hardscape, plants, and irrigation runs $9,500–$20,000. Always get 3 quotes and a written scope before starting.

faqs.question: How much does sprinkler/irrigation installation cost in Seattle?
faqs.answer: Sprinkler system installation in Seattle runs $2,800–$7,000, averaging $4,500 for a standard residential lot. Smart controllers add $150–$400 but can reduce water bills 20–40%. faqs.question: How much does tree trimming cost in Seattle?
faqs.answer: Tree trimming in Seattle costs $320–$420 for small trees (under 30 ft) and $950–$1250 for large trees. Emergency tree removal after storms runs higher — $500–$2,500 depending on size and accessibility. Always verify tree service companies carry adequate liability insurance before hiring.

faqs.question: How often should I have my lawn mowed in Seattle?
faqs.answer: In Seattle, march–november is the active growing season. During peak growth (spring/summer), weekly mowing keeps lawns healthy and prevents scalping. Bi-weekly works during slower growth periods. Seattle's mild climate extends the growing season. Moss, rain garden management, and drought-tolerant tree-service are common requests. Most Seattle tree-service companies offer flexible weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly plans.

faqs.question: What's the best time to do tree-service in Seattle?
faqs.answer: The best time for major tree-service projects in Seattle is early spring (March–April) or early fall (September–October) when temperatures are moderate and plants establish well. Seattle's mild climate extends the growing season. Moss, rain garden management, and drought-tolerant tree-service are common requests. Booking early in spring is critical — most reputable Seattle landscapers are booked 3–6 weeks out by May.

faqs.question: How do I choose a tree-service company in Seattle?
faqs.answer: Look for Seattle landscapers who: (1) are licensed and carry general liability + workers' comp, (2) have 4.5+ star ratings with 50+ reviews, (3) provide written quotes with a defined scope of work, (4) have been operating locally for 5+ years, (5) offer a satisfaction guarantee. Avoid cash-only contractors or those who can't provide a certificate of insurance.

faqs.question: Do tree-service companies in Seattle offer seasonal contracts?
faqs.answer: Yes — most Seattle tree-service companies offer seasonal or annual maintenance contracts that bundle mowing, edging, fertilizing, mulching, and cleanup. Annual contracts typically save 10–20% vs. per-service pricing and guarantee priority scheduling. Ask about start-of-season aeration and overseeding packages, which are popular in Seattle's climate.

buyerTips: Always get 3 written quotes before hiring a landscaper in Seattle — prices for the same job vary 25–40%.
Verify the company carries general liability insurance ($1M minimum) and workers' comp — accidents on your property without coverage can become your liability.
Ask for references from Seattle homeowners who had similar work done — a reputable landscaper will provide them without hesitation.
Get everything in writing: scope of work, materials, plant species/varieties, timeline, and payment schedule.
For new plantings, ask about the warranty — good Seattle landscapers guarantee plants for 1 growing season.
Book spring projects early (January–February) — the best Seattle landscapers fill their calendars by March.
Annual maintenance contracts save 10–20% over one-off services and guarantee consistent quality throughout the season.
